commit.message:
fix: stream image upload via stdin to avoid curl OOM on large files

--data-binary @file loads the entire file into memory before sending,
which fails with 'out of memory' for large images (1.2G+).

Fix: use --data-binary @- and redirect the file via stdin, which makes
curl stream the data without buffering the whole file in memory.

Applied to both harvester-vm.sh (_do_upload) and image/main.tf (terraform_data).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(wasm): append QWT_CONFIG -= directives instead of sed-deleting lines

The sed-delete approach (sed -i '/QwtConcurrent/d') was unreliable:
qmake sub-directory builds re-read qwtconfig.pri fresh, but multi-
feature lines or includes from other .pri files could still add the
feature back. Appending 'QWT_CONFIG -= <feat>' at the END of
qwtconfig.pri is guaranteed to win because qmake processes the file
sequentially — a trailing -= always overrides any earlier +=.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: strip surrounding quotes from kubeconfig server/token values

The _kc_field() regex \S+ matched the surrounding YAML quotes when the
kubeconfig value was written as:
  server: "https://harvester-hpc.edge.suse.org/k8s/clusters/local"

This caused the extracted server value to be
  "https://..." (with literal quote characters)
producing a malformed curl URL and HTTP code 000 on every poll,
so _do_upload() always timed out after 5 minutes.

Fix: strip leading/trailing quote characters (", ') from the
extracted value in both the Python3 path (.strip('"\''))) and the
awk fallback (gsub(/["\047]/,"",...)).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix outcome_judge prompt-schema mismatch

Three issues:
1. Prompt used 'CRITICAL SUCCESS' / 'CRITICAL FAILURE' labels but schema
   literals are 'critical' / 'failure' — LLM could output 'critical_failure'
   and fail Pydantic validation.
2. Schema has 'partial' success_level but prompt had zero definition of it
   (no roll threshold, no should_succeed guidance) — LLM guessed randomly.
3. should_succeed field is required but prompt never mentioned it; unclear
   for partial outcomes.

Fix: replace the outcome table with one that maps roll results directly to
schema literal strings, defines partial as roll >= DC-4, clarifies
should_succeed rule (true for critical/success/partial, false for failure),
and notes natural 1 maps to 'failure'. Updated field description to match.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(web): harden SignalR and JS interop for circuit-disconnect resilience

- CanvasInteropService: Add JSDisconnectedException + TaskCanceledException
  handling to all 5 interop methods (previously only DisposeAsync was guarded)
- CanvasInteropService: Add ILogger for prerender-skip diagnostics
- SystemMonitorService: Error-isolated event invocation via GetInvocationList
  pattern (prevents one subscriber from killing others on timer thread)
- TradingHub: Log disconnection exceptions at Warning level (was silent)

feat(seatbelt): introduce hedging resilience middleware (#298)

## Hedging Middleware

Adds a **hedging** resilience middleware to `seatbelt` that reduces tail
latency by launching additional concurrent requests when the original is
slow. The first acceptable result wins; remaining in-flight requests are
  cancelled.

   ### How it works

   1. The original request is sent immediately.
2. After a configurable delay, additional hedging attempts launch
concurrently.
3. A user-provided recovery callback classifies each result —
non-recoverable results are returned immediately.

   ### Hedging delay

   | Method | Behavior |
   |--------|----------|
| **`hedging_delay(duration)`** | Waits a fixed duration before each
hedging attempt *(default: 500ms)* |
| **`hedging_delay(Duration::ZERO)`** | Launches all hedging attempts at
once |
| **`hedging_delay_with(fn)`** | Computes delay per attempt via callback
|

   ### Configuration

Uses a **type-state builder** pattern — `clone_input` and `recovery` are
enforced at compile time before the layer can be constructed.

   ```rust
   let stack = (
       Hedging::layer("my_hedging", &context)
           .clone_input()
           .recovery_with(|result, _| match result {
               Ok(_) => RecoveryInfo::never(),
               Err(_) => RecoveryInfo::retry(),
           })
           .hedging_delay(Duration::from_secs(1))
           .max_hedged_attempts(2),
       Execute::new(my_operation),
   );

Runtime configuration is also supported via HedgingConfig
(serde-compatible):

   .config(&HedgingConfig {
       enabled: true,
       hedging_delay: Duration::from_millis(500),
       max_hedged_attempts: 1,
   })
```

commit.message:
fix: deploy not applying — clear WEBSITE_RUN_FROM_PACKAGE + force restart (#1024)

Root cause: az webapp up (the old deploy command) sets WEBSITE_RUN_FROM_PACKAGE=1,
which makes the app run from a cached package. When we switched to
az webapp deploy --type zip, the zip was extracted to wwwroot but the
app continued running from the old package.

Fix applied to all 7 deploy workflows (9 deploy sites):
- Set WEBSITE_RUN_FROM_PACKAGE=0 before each deploy
- Add --clean true to az webapp deploy to purge stale files
- Explicit az webapp restart after deploy to ensure new binary loads

Co-authored-by: root <root@localhost.localdomain>
